name: Sun Loan Company
description_short: Sun Loan Company Albuquerque, New Mexico — Sun Loan Company offers personal installment loans ($150–$3,999) and tax preparation services at 40+ location...
description_long:
Sun Loan Company has operated for over 30 years as a regional personal loan lender with a physical branch presence across multiple states including Nevada, Texas, Oklahoma, Tennessee, Missouri, Illinois, and Alabama. The company positions itself as a community-focused alternative to traditional banks, emphasizing accessibility and customer service rather than strict credit-based lending. They offer personal installment loans ranging from $150 to $3,999.15 in Nevada, with same-day or next-day funding available through in-person pickup or bank deposit. Beyond loans, Sun Loan provides tax preparation services, including refund advances up to $7,000 (filing by February 28, 2025), plus ancillary services like check cashing, faxing, and copies. The Las Vegas branch is located at 1600 N. Nellis Boulevard and operates Monday–Friday 8:30 AM–5:30 PM, with limited Saturday hours. Sun Loan distinguishes itself through: (1) evaluating applicants on factors beyond credit score, (2) offering online applications that don't affect credit scores, (3) same-day funding capability, (4) bilingual Spanish-language services, and (5) integrated tax preparation as a secondary revenue stream. Customer reviews consistently praise staff friendliness and quick processing. However, the company is a traditional installment lender, not a payday lender, and loan amounts in Nevada max out under $4,000—suitable for small-to-medium emergency needs but not larger consolidation or purchase loans. No APR or fee information is disclosed on the website, making rate transparency difficult to assess.

As a financial institution, this lender competes with both traditional banks and newer fintech personal loan lenders in the consumer lending space. Borrowers seeking personal loans for bad credit may find more flexible terms through online lenders, while those focused on simplifying payments may benefit from debt consolidation loans with fixed rates. For credit building, secured credit cards and credit builder loans offer structured paths to improvement. Credit monitoring services provide ongoing visibility into credit health, and credit counseling through nonprofit agencies can help consumers create sustainable budgeting plans. Many of these lenders offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments over 12 to 60 months, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Same-day or next-day funding available through in-person or bank deposit
Loan approval considers factors beyond credit score, not just FICO
Online application process does not trigger a hard credit inquiry
Loan amounts up to $3,999.15 available in Nevada for quick access
Spanish-language services offered ("Ofrecemos servicios en español")
Integrated tax preparation with refund advances up to $7,000
40+ year operating history and multi-state presence indicates stability
cons: No APR, interest rate, or fee information disclosed on website—rates are opaque
Maximum loan amount of $3,999.15 in Nevada is relatively low for larger needs
Limited to 7 states (Nevada, Texas, Oklahoma, Tennessee, Missouri, Illinois, Alabama)—not national
No information on minimum credit score requirements or specific approval criteria
Customer reviews appear selectively displayed and may not reflect full customer experience
best_for: Consumers with fair or poor credit who need $500–$3,500 quickly and want alternatives to payday lenders
Individuals seeking tax preparation and refund advances alongside personal loan options
Borrowers in Nevada, Texas, Oklahoma, Tennessee, Missouri, Illinois, or Alabama who prefer in-person interaction
Spanish-speaking consumers who need bilingual financial services
diagnosis: Sun Loan Company is best for borrowers in its 7-state footprint who need $500–$3,999 quickly and value in-person service and non-traditional credit evaluation. The major caveat is complete lack of transparency on APR and fees—applicants must visit a branch or call (800) 786-5266 to learn actual costs, making online rate comparison impossible.
